Code fragment: public final TableField<StripeHppRequestsRecord, ULong> RECORD_ID = createField(DSL.name("record_id"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.BIGINTUNSIGNED.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (RECORD_ID) even though it already has a natural unique key on SESSION_ID (KEY_STRIPE_HPP_REQUESTS_STRIPE_HPP_REQUESTS_KB_SESSION_ID). Consider using SESSION_ID as the primary key since it uniquely identifies each record and is a stable business identifier.

Code fragment: public final TableField<StripePaymentMethodsRecord, ULong> RECORD_ID = createField(DSL.name("record_id"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.BIGINTUNSIGNED.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");

Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the STRIPE_HPP_REQUESTS table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed for this operation.
Using fetchOne() with a table reference fetches all columns from STRIPE_RESPONSES. Consider using selectFrom() with explicit column selection or select() to retrieve only the required columns.
